macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Massive Deviation in Stocks Signals Potential 15% Pullback
items.summary: Portfolio manager Lance Roberts warns of a high risk of a stock market pullback of up to 15% due to a massive deviation between current prices and daily moving averages following a rapid rally. Discussion also covers non-farm payrolls, UMICH consumer sentiment, bond yields, and recent trades.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Sees $202 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced $202 million in total liquidations, with shorts accounting for $153 million and longs $49 million; Bitcoin and Ethereum saw significant short liquidations, and 81,778 traders were liquidated globally.

items.title: BlackRock Submits $10 Billion in New Tokenization Fund Filings
items.summary: BlackRock has filed for $10 billion in new blockchain fund applications, bringing $7 billion in ETH-linked assets on-chain as part of its deepening push into tokenization.

items.title: Jupiter (JUP) Surges 25% on Binance Wallet Integration
items.summary: Jupiter's JUP token surged 16.9-25% in 24 hours due to increased user activity and integration with Binance Wallet, expanding its lending utility; Solana ETFs saw $6.23 million inflows.

items.title: AERO Surges 10% After Massive Short Liquidations
items.summary: Aerodrome Finance's AERO token rose 10.99% with trading volume exploding 98%, triggered by $12.71K in short liquidations across exchanges like Binance and Bybit.

items.title: Bitcoin Open Interest Surpasses 2025 All-Time Highs
items.summary: Bitcoin's open interest has exploded beyond peak levels from 2025's all-time high, indicating heightened derivatives activity amid BTC trading around $80,000.

items.title: Bybit Launches 24/7 Perpetual Contracts for US Stocks and ETFs
items.summary: Bybit expanded its offerings with perpetual contracts for dozens of US stocks and global ETFs, including AI-infrastructure assets, enabling 24/7 TradFi trading.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Realtime-2 with Advanced Voice Reasoning and Live Translation
items.summary: OpenAI added three new realtime audio models to its API: GPT-Realtime-2, GPT-Realtime-Translate, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper, with GPT-Realtime-2 featuring GPT-5-class reasoning for handling complex requests and natural conversations.

items.title: GPT-5.5 Tops Leaderboards in Coding, Visual Puzzles, and Intelligence but Struggles with Hallucinations
items.summary: OpenAI's latest flagship model update sets new benchmarks in key areas but has challenges distinguishing known from unknown information.

items.title: Akamai Secures $1.8B Seven-Year Deal to Host Anthropic's LLMs
items.summary: Akamai announced its largest-ever contract with Anthropic for AI workloads, emphasizing scale, performance, and reliability; revenue will ramp up later this year on a consumption basis.

items.title: ByteDance Launches Seedance 2.0 Video Model in CapCut as OpenAI Shuts Down Sora
items.summary: ByteDance integrated its state-of-the-art video generation model into CapCut, reaching hundreds of millions of users, while OpenAI prepares to discontinue Sora.

items.title: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.6 Matches Top Open Models in Coding and Multi-Agent Tasks
items.summary: The updated Kimi model rivals Qwen3.6 Max and DeepSeek V4, with improvements in long autonomous coding and scaled multi-agent orchestration.

items.title: Z.ai Releases GLM 5.1 for Autonomous Long-Running Tasks Up to Eight Hours
items.summary: The open-weights model evaluates interim results and adjusts approaches hundreds of times for complex single tasks.

items.title: Eli Lilly Invests $2.75B in Insilico for AI-Driven Drug Development
items.summary: The pharmaceutical giant bets big on generative AI's potential to produce drugs, following successes in text, images, and code.

items.title: Google Internally Tests Gemini-Powered 'Remy' Personal AI Agent
items.summary: Remy is a 24/7 agent for work, school, and life that takes actions on behalf of users, currently in staff-only testing.

items.title: Meta Pivots from Llama with Closed Muse Spark Model
items.summary: Meta shifts away from open-weights strategy to launch a closed alternative.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Plan 8 MMO Looter Shooter: What Happened to Pearl Abyss' Ambitious Project?
items.summary: Pearl Abyss' Plan 8, an ambitious MMO looter shooter featuring exosuits and open-world combat, has gone silent for years after being announced in 2019 alongside Crimson Desert and DokeV. The game was set to use the Black Space Engine but has seen minimal updates since its reveal.

items.title: The Division 2: Bloody Red Hunter Returns with New Title Update
items.summary: The Division 2 receives a weekend news update featuring the return of the Bloody Red Hunter and a new title update with upcoming content expectations.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Nintendo Raises Switch 2 Prices Amid Chip Crisis
items.summary: Nintendo is increasing Switch 2 prices as chip supply challenges impact production costs, despite selling 19.86 million units last year and forecasting lower sales next year.

items.title: Intel Signs Preliminary Deal to Produce Chips for Apple
items.summary: Intel has reportedly signed a preliminary agreement to manufacture chips for Apple, marking a significant shift in Apple's semiconductor sourcing strategy.

items.title: Sony Partners with TSMC on New Image Sensor Facility
items.summary: Sony is teaming up with chip manufacturing giant TSMC to establish a new facility for producing advanced image sensors.

items.title: NASA to Begin Training with Blue Origin's Crew Moon Lander
items.summary: NASA is starting training with a prototype of Blue Origin's crew moon lander as the space agency prepares to put astronauts on the moon in 2028.

items.title: Lime Files for IPO
items.summary: The electric scooter rental company Lime has filed for an initial public offering.

items.title: Scientists Achieve Unhackable Quantum Key Distribution Across 120 Kilometers
items.summary: An international research team demonstrated a stable quantum encryption system using semiconductor quantum dots that achieved one of the highest secure key rates while operating continuously for over six hours.

items.title: Meta Removes End-to-End Encryption from Instagram Direct Messages
items.summary: Instagram is shutting down end-to-end encryption for direct messages, giving Meta access to chat content.

items.title: Apple AirPods with Built-in Camera in Final Testing Stage
items.summary: According to analyst Mark Gurman, Apple's new AirPods featuring an integrated camera are currently in the final testing phase.
